description_long: This is a classic pith helmet, also known as a safari helmet, made from a light tan felt material. The hat features a rounded, domed crown with a small button-like knob at the very top center. Around the lower part of the crown, there is a wide, ribbed band that encircles the hat, providing both structural reinforcement and a subtle visual accent. The brim is moderately wide, gently sloping downward all around, offering sun protection for the wearer. The surface texture appears slightly rough and matte, typical of felted or pressed fiber construction. There are no visible decorative elements, logos, or additional adornments on the exterior.
description: A tan felt pith helmet with a domed crown, ribbed band, and gently sloping brim, featuring a small knob at the top and a matte, textured finish.
description_view_0: Side view showing the rounded crown, ribbed band, and the downward-sloping brim with the top knob visible.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the symmetrical, pointed front brim and the central seam running up the crown.
description_view_2: Opposite side view similar to view 0, showing the continuous ribbed band and the smooth, unadorned surface.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the back seam of the crown and the even, rounded shape of the brim.
attribute_crown_shape: rounded, domed crown with central knob
